---
name: ux-refactor
description: "Refactor UI/UX design ONLY — no logic, no state, no API changes. Use when improving visual consistency, fixing layout symmetry, removing unnecessary scroll, standardizing spacing, or making views look like premium enterprise software. Covers glassmorphism, spatial rhythm, viewport-locked layouts, responsive adaptation, and design consistency across views."
argument-hint: "View or component to refactor (e.g., BillingView, DashboardCards, SettingsLayout)"
---

# UX/UI Spatial Design Refactoring

## Philosophy: Spatial Design Engineering

This is NOT decoration. This is **engineering visual space** with the same rigor as engineering code. Every pixel has a reason. Every spacing is derived from a system. The result must look like it was designed by a senior product designer at Stripe/Linear/Vercel — not generated by AI.

### Core Principles
1. **Viewport-locked** — the app fills the screen. Internal sections scroll, not the page.
2. **Spatial rhythm** — all spacing derives from a 4px base grid (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32).
3. **Optical symmetry** — what LOOKS centered/aligned, not just mathematically centered.
4. **Content density** — enterprise software is dense but readable. No wasted space.
5. **Consistent containment** — same card/panel treatment across ALL views.
6. **Typography hierarchy by weight** — use font-weight to create hierarchy, minimize size variation.
7. **Zero layout shift** — skeletons match final content dimensions exactly.
8. **Mobile: reflow, don't shrink** — content reflows to stack, never scales down.
9. **Choreographed motion** — every transition is intentional, consistent, and physically grounded.
10. **12-column spatial grid** — content regions align to a 12-column invisible grid for optical harmony.

## CRITICAL RULES

### What This Skill DOES
- Change CSS classes, Tailwind utilities, layout structure
- Fix spacing, alignment, symmetry
- Standardize headers, cards, containers across views
- Remove unnecessary scroll, fix viewport utilization
- Add responsive breakpoints for mobile
- Fix visual inconsistencies between views
- Create/update shared layout primitives if they serve 3+ views

### What This Skill DOES NOT DO
- Change state management, hooks, or API calls
- Add/remove features or functionality
- Modify business logic or data flow
- Add new dependencies or libraries

## Design Tokens (Mandatory)

Tokens are **NOT just documentation** — they live as CSS custom properties in `src/app/globals.css` via Tailwind v4's `@theme inline`. This is the single source of truth. When this skill references a token, it MUST exist in globals.css.

### Token Implementation Location

All spatial design tokens go in `globals.css` inside the existing `@theme inline` block:

```css
/* src/app/globals.css — add to @theme inline {} */
@theme inline {
  /* ... existing shadcn tokens ... */

  /* ── Spatial Design Tokens ─────────────────────── */
  --spacing-xs: 4px;
  --spacing-sm: 8px;
  --spacing-md: 16px;
  --spacing-lg: 24px;
  --spacing-xl: 32px;

  --content-max: 1440px;
  --sidebar-collapsed: 80px;
  --sidebar-expanded: 240px;

  --radius-card: 16px;
  --radius-button: 12px;
  --radius-input: 8px;

  --duration-fast: 150ms;
  --duration-default: 200ms;
  --duration-emphasis: 300ms;
  --ease-default: cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1);
  --ease-emphasis: cubic-bezier(0.43, 0.13, 0.23, 0.96);

  /* ── Glassmorphism Tokens ──────────────────────── */
  --glass-bg: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4);
  --glass-border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);
  --glass-border-hover: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2);
  --glass-surface: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.02);
  --glass-elevated: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6);
  --glass-divider: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05);
}

.dark {
  /* dark glassmorphism already defined above (default) */
}

:root {
  /* light mode overrides */
  --glass-bg: rgba(255, 255, 255, 1);
  --glass-border: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
  --glass-border-hover: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.15);
  --glass-surface: rgba(249, 250, 251, 1);
  --glass-elevated: rgba(255, 255, 255, 1);
  --glass-divider: rgba(229, 231, 235, 1);
}
```

This means Tailwind classes like `max-w-(--content-max)`, `rounded-(--radius-card)`, `duration-(--duration-default)` all work natively. No JS token file needed.

### Spacing Scale (4px grid)
```
space-1:  4px  → gap-1, p-1
space-2:  8px  → gap-2, p-2
space-3: 12px  → gap-3, p-3    ← DEFAULT page edge padding
space-4: 16px  → gap-4, p-4    ← DEFAULT content/card padding
space-5: 20px  → gap-5, p-5
space-6: 24px  → gap-6, p-6    ← Section separation
space-8: 32px  → gap-8, p-8    ← Major section separation
```

### Container & Grid System
```
── Widths ───────────────────────────────────────────
Sidebar collapsed:  80px  (w-20)
Sidebar expanded:  240px  (w-60)
Content max:      1440px  (max-w-[1440px] mx-auto)

── 12-Column Grid ───────────────────────────────────
Full:        col-span-12                    (table views, editors)
3/4 + 1/4:  col-span-9 + col-span-3        (content + sidebar)
2/3 + 1/3:  col-span-8 + col-span-4        (main + panel)
Half:        col-span-6 + col-span-6        (split views)
Thirds:      col-span-4 × 3                (card grids)
Quarters:    col-span-3 × 4                (stat cards)
```

**Grid wrapper** (use on any mixed-content page):
```tsx
<div className="grid grid-cols-12 gap-6">
  <div className="col-span-12 lg:col-span-8">{/* main */}</div>
  <div className="col-span-12 lg:col-span-4">{/* sidebar */}</div>
</div>
```

### Border Radius
```
Cards/Panels:  rounded-2xl  (16px)
Buttons:       rounded-xl   (12px)
Badges/Tags:   rounded-full
Inputs:        rounded-lg   (8px)
```

### Glassmorphism Tokens
```
── Dark Mode (Primary) ──────────────────────────────
Card:       bg-black/40 backdrop-blur-xl border border-white/10 rounded-2xl
Card hover: hover:border-white/20 hover:shadow-[0_0_30px_rgba(255,255,255,0.03)]
Surface:    bg-white/[0.02]
Elevated:   bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-2xl border-white/10
Divider:    border-white/5

── Light Mode ───────────────────────────────────────
Card:       bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm rounded-2xl
Card hover: hover:border-gray-300 hover:shadow-md
Surface:    bg-gray-50
Elevated:   bg-white shadow-lg border-gray-200
Divider:    border-gray-200
```

### Typography Scale
```
Page title:    text-sm  font-bold  tracking-tight   (ONE per view, 14px)
Section title: text-base font-semibold               (cards, panels)
Body:          text-sm  font-normal                   (default)
Label:         text-xs  font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-gray-500
Caption:       text-xs  font-normal text-gray-500
Mono/Code:     text-xs  font-mono  (Geist Mono)
```

### Color Semantics
```
Accent:    cyan-400 / cyan-500      (links, active states, highlights)
Success:   emerald-400 / emerald-500
Warning:   amber-400 / amber-500
Error:     red-400 / red-500
Muted:     gray-500 (dark) / gray-400 (light)
```

### Motion System

Every transition must feel **physical** — no instant pops, no rubber-band bounces.

```
── Timing Curves ───────────────────────────────────
Default:     transition-all duration-200 ease-out
Emphasis:    transition-all duration-300 ease-[cubic-bezier(0.43,0.13,0.23,0.96)]
Spring:      Framer Motion spring (stiffness: 300, damping: 30)
Quick:       transition-all duration-150 ease-out

── Where to Apply ──────────────────────────────────
Hover states:    duration-200 ease-out  (bg, border, shadow, scale)
Dropdowns:       duration-200 ease-out  (opacity + translateY 4px)
Modals:          duration-300 ease-out  (opacity + scale from 0.95)
Sidebar:         spring animation        (Framer Motion, already exists)
Tab switch:      duration-200 ease-out  (opacity crossfade)
Cards:           duration-200 ease-out  (border-color, shadow)
Page enter:      duration-300 ease-out  (opacity + translateY 8px)  [animate-in]

── Scale on Hover ──────────────────────────────────
Stat cards:      hover:scale-[1.02]     (subtle lift)
Action cards:    hover:scale-105        (clickable emphasis)
Buttons:         NO scale — use bg/shadow only
Table rows:      NO scale — use bg change only

── Rules ───────────────────────────────────────────
NEVER: duration-500+, bounce, elastic, spring on small elements
NEVER: animate layout shifts (width/height changes on click)
NEVER: animate during scroll (use IntersectionObserver for reveal only)
ALWAYS: reduce-motion media query respected (prefers-reduced-motion)
```

## Density Modes

Enterprise SaaS users want control over information density. The system supports 3 modes applied via a CSS class on `<html>` or a React context:

```
── Mode ──────── Row Height ── Card Padding ── Body Font ── Gap ──
comfortable       44px (h-11)    p-5            text-sm      gap-6
default           36px (h-9)     p-4            text-sm      gap-4
compact           28px (h-7)     p-3            text-xs      gap-3
```

### Implementation Pattern

**Location**: `src/components/layout/DensityProvider.tsx`

```tsx
type Density = "comfortable" | "default" | "compact";

const DensityContext = createContext<{
  density: Density;
  setDensity: (d: Density) => void;
}>({ density: "default", setDensity: () => {} });

export function DensityProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [density, setDensity] = useState<Density>("default");
  return (
    <DensityContext.Provider value={{ density, setDensity }}>
      <div data-density={density}>{children}</div>
    </DensityContext.Provider>
  );
}

export const useDensity = () => useContext(DensityContext);
```

**CSS tokens** (add to `globals.css`):
```css
[data-density="comfortable"] {
  --row-height: 44px;
  --cell-padding: 20px;
  --section-gap: 24px;
}
[data-density="default"] {
  --row-height: 36px;
  --cell-padding: 16px;
  --section-gap: 16px;
}
[data-density="compact"] {
  --row-height: 28px;
  --cell-padding: 12px;
  --section-gap: 12px;
}
```

**Usage in components**: `style={{ height: 'var(--row-height)' }}` or utility classes `h-(--row-height) p-(--cell-padding) gap-(--section-gap)`.

**Density toggle**: small icon button in app header (3 horizontal lines with different spacing). Persisted in `localStorage`.

**Rule**: Density ONLY changes spacing/sizing. NEVER changes colors, fonts, glassmorphism, or layout structure.

## Layout Primitives

These are **shared components** (`src/components/layout/`) that enforce structural consistency across 20+ views. They handle ONLY layout — zero logic, zero data fetching.

### PageLayout — The Universal Page Wrapper
Every page view MUST use this structure. If these components don't exist yet, create them.

**Location**: `src/components/layout/PageLayout.tsx`

```tsx
// PageLayout — wraps every view. Enforces viewport-lock + flex column.
interface PageLayoutProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
}
export function PageLayout({ children }: PageLayoutProps) {
  return <div className="h-full flex flex-col">{children}</div>;
}

// PageHeader — shrink-0, consistent height across ALL views.
interface PageHeaderProps {
  title: string;
  subtitle?: string;
  actions?: React.ReactNode;
}
export function PageHeader({ title, subtitle, actions }: PageHeaderProps) {
  const { theme } = useTheme();
  const isDark = theme === "dark";
  return (
    <div className="shrink-0 px-3 py-3 flex items-center justify-between">
      <div>
        <h1 className={`text-sm font-bold tracking-tight ${isDark ? "text-white" : "text-gray-900"}`}>
          {title}
        </h1>
        {subtitle && (
          <p className={`text-xs mt-0.5 ${isDark ? "text-gray-500" : "text-gray-500"}`}>{subtitle}</p>
        )}
      </div>
      {actions && <div className="flex items-center gap-3">{actions}</div>}
    </div>
  );
}

// PageContent — flex-1, only THIS area scrolls.
interface PageContentProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  noPadding?: boolean;  // for full-bleed table views
}
export function PageContent({ children, noPadding }: PageContentProps) {
  return (
    <div className={`flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-auto ${noPadding ? "" : "px-3 pb-3"}`}>
      {children}
    </div>
  );
}

// PageFooter — shrink-0, optional.
interface PageFooterProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
}
export function PageFooter({ children }: PageFooterProps) {
  const { theme } = useTheme();
  const isDark = theme === "dark";
  return (
    <div className={`shrink-0 px-3 py-2 border-t ${isDark ? "border-white/5 bg-white/[0.02]" : "border-gray-200 bg-gray-50"}`}>
      {children}
    </div>
  );
}

// PageBanner — bold branded header for feature landing pages.
// Use for Billing, Licensing, and any feature that needs a prominent title.
interface PageBannerProps {
  title: string;
  subtitle?: string;
  actions?: React.ReactNode;
}
export function PageBanner({ title, subtitle, actions }: PageBannerProps) {
  const { theme } = useTheme();
  const isDark = theme === "dark";
  return (
    <div className={`sticky top-0 z-30 shrink-0 backdrop-blur-xl border-b transition-colors duration-200 ${
      isDark ? "bg-black/50 border-white/5" : "bg-white/70 border-gray-100"
    }`}>
      <div className="px-3 py-4 flex items-center justify-between">
        <div>
          <h1 className={`text-2xl font-black tracking-tighter uppercase italic ${
            isDark
              ? "bg-linear-to-r from-white via-white to-white/40 bg-clip-text text-transparent"
              : "text-gray-900"
          }`}>
            {title}
          </h1>
          {subtitle && (
            <p className={`text-xs font-mono tracking-widest uppercase mt-1 ${
              isDark ? "text-cyan-400/60" : "text-blue-600/60"
            }`}>{subtitle}</p>
          )}
        </div>
        {actions && <div className="flex items-center gap-3">{actions}</div>}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### When to use each header

| Component | Style | Use for |
|-----------|-------|---------|
| `PageHeader` | `text-sm font-bold` (14px, clean) | Internal views, dashboards, settings, tables |
| `PageBanner` | `text-2xl font-black uppercase italic` (gradient) | Feature landing pages: Billing, Licensing, Marketplace |

**PageHeader usage** (compact, functional):
```tsx
<PageLayout>
  <PageHeader title="Command Center" subtitle="SYS D2199802 · System Healthy" actions={<RunDiagnostics />} />
  <PageContent>...</PageContent>
</PageLayout>
```

**PageBanner usage** (bold, branded):
```tsx
<PageLayout>
  <PageBanner title="Billing Portal" subtitle="Subscription & Payment Management" actions={<TrustBadges />} />
  <PageContent>...</PageContent>
</PageLayout>
```

### TableChrome — Standard Table Wrapper
Every DataTable view uses this same chrome. Enforces consistent search bar, filters, and footer.

**Location**: `src/components/layout/TableChrome.tsx`

```tsx
interface TableChromeProps {
  toolbar?: React.ReactNode;      // search, filters, action buttons
  children: React.ReactNode;      // <DataTable ... />
  footer?: React.ReactNode;       // "Showing X of Y" or pagination info
}
export function TableChrome({ toolbar, children, footer }: TableChromeProps) {
  const { theme } = useTheme();
  const isDark = theme === "dark";
  const card = isDark
    ? "bg-linear-to-br from-[#0a0a0a]/80 to-gray-900/50 border-white/10"
    : "bg-white border-gray-200 shadow-sm";
  const border = isDark ? "border-white/5" : "border-gray-200";

  return (
    <div className={`h-full flex flex-col rounded-2xl border overflow-hidden ${card}`}>
      {toolbar && (
        <div className={`shrink-0 px-4 py-3 border-b ${border}`}>{toolbar}</div>
      )}
      <div className="flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-auto">{children}</div>
      {footer && (
        <div className={`shrink-0 px-4 py-3 border-t ${border}`}>{footer}</div>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
```

**Usage**:
```tsx
<TableChrome
  toolbar={<SearchInput value={search} onChange={setSearch} />}
  footer={<p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Showing {items.length} of {total} entries</p>}
>
  <DataTable columns={columns} data={items} isLoading={isLoading}
    totalItems={total} hasNextPage={hasNextPage}
    isFetchingNextPage={isFetchingNextPage} onLoadMore={fetchNextPage} />
</TableChrome>
```

## Form Layout System

Forms are 30-40% of SaaS interactions. They need the same structural rigor as tables and dashboards.

### Form Primitives

**Location**: `src/components/layout/FormLayout.tsx`

```tsx
// FormLayout — wraps an entire form view. Constrains width, centers content.
interface FormLayoutProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  maxWidth?: "sm" | "md" | "lg";  // sm=480, md=640, lg=768
}
export function FormLayout({ children, maxWidth = "md" }: FormLayoutProps) {
  const widths = { sm: "max-w-[480px]", md: "max-w-[640px]", lg: "max-w-[768px]" };
  return <div className={`${widths[maxWidth]} mx-auto w-full space-y-6`}>{children}</div>;
}

// FormSection — groups related fields with a label. Visual separator.
interface FormSectionProps {
  title: string;
  description?: string;
  children: React.ReactNode;
}
export function FormSection({ title, description, children }: FormSectionProps) {
  const { theme } = useTheme();
  const isDark = theme === "dark";
  return (
    <div className={`pb-6 border-b ${isDark ? "border-white/5" : "border-gray-200"}`}>
      <div className="mb-4">
        <h3 className={`text-base font-semibold ${isDark ? "text-white" : "text-gray-900"}`}>{title}</h3>
        {description && (
          <p className={`text-xs mt-1 ${isDark ? "text-gray-500" : "text-gray-500"}`}>{description}</p>
        )}
      </div>
      <div className="space-y-4">{children}</div>
    </div>
  );
}

// FieldGroup — horizontal row of fields (e.g., first name + last name).
interface FieldGroupProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  columns?: 2 | 3;  // default 2
}
export function FieldGroup({ children, columns = 2 }: FieldGroupProps) {
  return (
    <div className={`grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-${columns} gap-4`}>{children}</div>
  );
}
```

### Form View Pattern
```tsx
<PageLayout>
  <PageHeader title="Organization Settings" subtitle="Update your organization details" />
  <PageContent>
    <FormLayout maxWidth="md">
      <FormSection title="General" description="Basic organization information">
        <FieldGroup>
          <InputField label="Name" ... />
          <InputField label="Slug" ... />
        </FieldGroup>
        <InputField label="Description" ... />
      </FormSection>
      <FormSection title="Billing" description="Payment and invoice settings">
        <InputField label="Billing Email" ... />
        <FieldGroup columns={3}>
          <SelectField label="Currency" ... />
          <SelectField label="Tax Rate" ... />
          <SelectField label="Region" ... />
        </FieldGroup>
      </FormSection>
    </FormLayout>
  </PageContent>
  <PageFooter>
    <div className="flex justify-end gap-3">
      <Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>
      <Button>Save Changes</Button>
    </div>
  </PageFooter>
</PageLayout>
```

### Form Rules
- Form max width: ALWAYS constrained (`max-w-[640px]` default). Never full-width forms.
- Labels: `text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-gray-500` (matches Table header style)
- Inputs: `rounded-lg` (8px), consistent height `h-9` (default density)
- Field spacing: `space-y-4` within a section
- Section spacing: `space-y-6` between sections, separated by `border-b`
- Actions (Save/Cancel): ALWAYS in `PageFooter`, right-aligned
- Validation errors: `text-xs text-red-400` below the input, no modals or toasts for field errors

## Empty / Loading / Error States

Every data-dependent view MUST handle 3 states with consistent visual treatment. No blank screens, no raw error strings.

### State Components

**Location**: `src/components/layout/ViewStates.tsx`

```tsx
// EmptyState — shown when a query returns 0 results.
interface EmptyStateProps {
  icon: React.ReactNode;       // Phosphor icon, size 48
  title: string;               // e.g., "No invoices yet"
  description?: string;        // e.g., "Invoices will appear here after your first purchase"
  action?: React.ReactNode;    // optional CTA button
}
export function EmptyState({ icon, title, description, action }: EmptyStateProps) {
  const { theme } = useTheme();
  const isDark = theme === "dark";
  return (
    <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col items-center justify-center py-16 px-6 text-center">
      <div className={`mb-4 ${isDark ? "text-gray-600" : "text-gray-300"}`}>{icon}</div>
      <h3 className={`text-base font-semibold ${isDark ? "text-white" : "text-gray-900"}`}>{title}</h3>
      {description && (
        <p className={`text-sm mt-1 max-w-sm ${isDark ? "text-gray-500" : "text-gray-500"}`}>{description}</p>
      )}
      {action && <div className="mt-4">{action}</div>}
    </div>
  );
}

// ErrorState — shown when a query fails.
interface ErrorStateProps {
  title?: string;              // default: "Something went wrong"
  description?: string;        // the error message
  onRetry?: () => void;        // refetch callback
}
export function ErrorState({ title = "Something went wrong", description, onRetry }: ErrorStateProps) {
  const { theme } = useTheme();
  const isDark = theme === "dark";
  return (
    <div className="flex-1 flex flex-col items-center justify-center py-16 px-6 text-center">
      <div className={`mb-4 ${isDark ? "text-red-400" : "text-red-500"}`}>
        <WarningCircle size={48} />
      </div>
      <h3 className={`text-base font-semibold ${isDark ? "text-white" : "text-gray-900"}`}>{title}</h3>
      {description && (
        <p className={`text-sm mt-1 max-w-sm ${isDark ? "text-gray-500" : "text-gray-500"}`}>{description}</p>
      )}
      {onRetry && (
        <button onClick={onRetry}
          className="mt-4 text-sm font-medium text-cyan-400 hover:text-cyan-300 transition-colors duration-200">
          Try again
        </button>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

// LoadingState — full-area skeleton, NOT a centered spinner.
interface LoadingStateProps {
  variant: "table" | "cards" | "form" | "detail";
}
export function LoadingState({ variant }: LoadingStateProps) {
  // Renders skeleton shapes matching the expected final layout.
  // Variant determines the skeleton pattern:
  //   table:  3-4 rows of horizontal bars
  //   cards:  grid of card-sized rectangles
  //   form:   stacked input-shaped bars
  //   detail: mixed content blocks
  // All skeletons use: bg-white/5 dark:bg-white/5 animate-pulse rounded-lg
  // Dimensions MUST match the real content to prevent layout shift.
}
```

### Usage Pattern
```tsx
function UsersView() {
  const { data, isLoading, isError, error, refetch } = useUsers();

  return (
    <PageLayout>
      <PageHeader title="Users" />
      <PageContent>
        {isLoading ? (
          <LoadingState variant="table" />
        ) : isError ? (
          <ErrorState description={error.message} onRetry={refetch} />
        ) : data.length === 0 ? (
          <EmptyState
            icon={<UsersThree size={48} />}
            title="No users yet"
            description="Invite team members to get started"
            action={<Button>Invite User</Button>}
          />
        ) : (
          <TableChrome ...>  <DataTable ... />  </TableChrome>
        )}
      </PageContent>
    </PageLayout>
  );
}
```

### State Rules
- **Loading**: ALWAYS skeletons (`animate-pulse` shapes), NEVER spinners or "Loading..." text
- **Empty**: ALWAYS icon (48px Phosphor) + title + description + optional CTA
- **Error**: ALWAYS retry button if the query supports refetch
- **Skeleton dimensions**: MUST match final content layout exactly (zero layout shift)
- **Placement**: States render INSIDE `<PageContent>` — header stays visible always

## Dashboard Content Patterns

### Stats Grid (always same structure)
```tsx
<div className="grid grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 mb-6">
  <StatCard label="Total Revenue" value="$12,340" delta="+12% from last month" trend="up" />
  <StatCard label="Active Users" value="1,234" delta="+3% from last month" trend="up" />
  {/* ... */}
</div>
```

Each StatCard internally:
```tsx
<div className="{glassmorphism-card} p-4 transition-all duration-200 ease-out hover:scale-[1.02]">
  <p className="text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-gray-500">{label}</p>
  <p className="text-2xl font-bold tabular-nums mt-1 {color}">{value}</p>
  <p className="text-xs text-gray-500 mt-1">{delta}</p>
</div>
```

### Mixed Content Page (stats + chart + table)
```tsx
<PageLayout>
  <PageHeader title="Analytics" subtitle="Performance overview" />
  <PageContent>
    {/* 12-col grid for spatial harmony */}
    <div className="grid grid-cols-12 gap-6">
      {/* Stats — full width */}
      <div className="col-span-12">
        <StatsGrid />
      </div>
      {/* Chart — 8 cols */}
      <div className="col-span-12 lg:col-span-8">
        <ChartCard />
      </div>
      {/* Side panel — 4 cols */}
      <div className="col-span-12 lg:col-span-4">
        <ActivityFeed />
      </div>
      {/* Table — full width */}
      <div className="col-span-12">
        <TableChrome toolbar={...} footer={...}>
          <DataTable ... />
        </TableChrome>
      </div>
    </div>
  </PageContent>
</PageLayout>
```

### Table-Only Page
```tsx
<PageLayout>
  <PageHeader title="Users" subtitle="Manage organization members" actions={<InviteButton />} />
  <PageContent noPadding>
    <TableChrome toolbar={<UserFilters />}
      footer={<p className="text-xs text-gray-500">Showing {len} of {total} entries</p>}>
      <DataTable ... />
    </TableChrome>
  </PageContent>
</PageLayout>
```

## Responsive Strategy

### Breakpoints
```
base:   Mobile (< 768px)  — Single column, stacked
md:     Tablet (768px+)   — 2 columns where needed
lg:     Desktop (1024px+) — Full layout with sidebar
xl:     Wide (1280px+)    — Optional wider content
```

### Mobile Adaptation Rules
- Sidebar: hidden by default, hamburger icon opens overlay drawer
- 12-col grid: all `lg:col-span-*` collapse to `col-span-12` on mobile
- Stats grid: `grid-cols-2` (never single column for stats)
- Tables: horizontal scroll via `overflow-x-auto` wrapper
- Cards: full width stack
- Padding: `px-4` on mobile, `px-6` on desktop (`px-4 md:px-6`)
- Modals: full screen on mobile (`fixed inset-0 md:relative md:max-w-lg md:rounded-2xl`)
- Page title: same `text-xl` — no shrinking text on mobile

## Procedure

### Step 1: Audit the Target View
Before changing ANYTHING:
1. Read the ENTIRE view file and all child components
2. Identify WHAT the view contains (stats? table? form? mixed?)
3. Check if `PageLayout`/`PageHeader`/`PageContent` exist in `src/components/layout/`
4. Compare against the Layout Primitives and Content Patterns above
5. List every inconsistency: wrong spacing, wrong typography, missing responsiveness, missing motion, scroll issues

### Step 2: Ensure Layout Primitives Exist
1. Check `src/components/layout/PageLayout.tsx` — if missing, create it with all 4 components
2. Check `src/components/layout/TableChrome.tsx` — if missing, create it
3. Check `src/components/layout/FormLayout.tsx` — if missing, create it (FormLayout, FormSection, FieldGroup)
4. Check `src/components/layout/ViewStates.tsx` — if missing, create it (EmptyState, ErrorState, LoadingState)
5. Check `src/components/layout/DensityProvider.tsx` — if missing, create it
6. Check `globals.css` for spatial design tokens in `@theme inline` — if missing, add them
7. These are created ONCE and used by ALL views

### Step 3: Refactor Layout Structure
Priority order:
1. **Wrap in PageLayout** — replace custom h-full/flex-col with `<PageLayout>`
2. **Use PageHeader** — replace custom header divs with `<PageHeader title="..." />`
3. **Use PageContent** — replace custom scroll containers with `<PageContent>`
4. **Use TableChrome** — replace custom table wrappers with `<TableChrome>`
5. **Apply 12-col grid** — wrap mixed content sections in `grid grid-cols-12 gap-6`

### Step 4: Standardize Tokens
1. **Glassmorphism** — same tokens for every card, no exceptions
2. **Typography** — apply scale. ONE `text-xl font-bold` per page.
3. **Spacing** — all from 4px grid. Check every `p-*`, `gap-*`, `m-*`.
4. **Motion** — add `transition-all duration-200 ease-out` on hover states, ensure cards use `hover:scale-[1.02]`
5. **Colors** — semantic only. No arbitrary hex values inline.
6. **CSS tokens** — verify spatial tokens exist in `globals.css @theme inline`. If not, add them.
7. **View states** — every data view must handle empty/loading/error with shared ViewStates components

### Step 5: Responsive Pass
1. Add 12-col grid responsive collapses (`lg:col-span-8` → `col-span-12` base)
2. Add mobile padding (`px-4 md:px-6`)
3. Ensure table has `overflow-x-auto` wrapper
4. Ensure modals work on mobile

### Step 6: Symmetry Check
Visually scan the view for:
- [ ] All cards same height in a row? (use grid, not flex-wrap)
- [ ] Header aligned with content below? (same `px-6`)
- [ ] Equal gaps between all sections? (consistent `gap-6`)
- [ ] Footer at bottom, not floating? (shrink-0 in flex-col)
- [ ] No orphaned extra whitespace anywhere?

### Step 7: Verify
- No page-level scroll (only PageContent scrolls)
- All spacing is from the 4px grid
- Typography follows the scale — no custom sizes
- All cards use identical glassmorphism tokens
- Motion is consistent (duration-200 ease-out everywhere)
- Dark/light mode both work
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit`

## Anti-patterns (NEVER DO)

### Layout
- ❌ `min-h-screen` on the root — causes page scroll
- ❌ Fixed heights on content areas (`h-[500px]`) — use flex-1
- ❌ Margin-based centering for layout — use flex/grid
- ❌ Different header heights across views — use PageHeader
- ❌ `scroll-smooth` or `overflow-y-scroll` on the page body
- ❌ Building a custom flex-col page wrapper instead of using PageLayout

### Spacing & Typography
- ❌ `text-3xl` or `text-4xl` for titles — too large for enterprise
- ❌ `py-8` or `py-10` spacing — wastes vertical space
- ❌ `space-y-8` between sections — too much gap, use `gap-6` max
- ❌ Arbitrary spacing (`mt-[13px]`, `p-[22px]`) — always use 4px grid

### Visual
- ❌ Different card styles in the same view
- ❌ Decorative gradients or shadows that serve no hierarchy purpose
- ❌ `duration-500` or slow animations — everything is 200ms max (300 for modals)
- ❌ Bounce or elastic easing — feels cheap, use ease-out
- ❌ Inline hex colors (`text-[#abc123]`) — use semantic tokens

### States
- ❌ Raw "Loading..." text or centered spinners — use skeleton LoadingState
- ❌ Blank screen when data is empty — use EmptyState component
- ❌ Showing error.message as raw text — use ErrorState with retry
- ❌ Empty states without an icon — always use 48px Phosphor icon

### Forms
- ❌ Full-width forms (no max-width constraint) — always use FormLayout
- ❌ Inconsistent field spacing — use space-y-4 within sections
- ❌ Save/Cancel buttons inside form body — always in PageFooter

### Process
- ❌ Changing ANY state, hooks, API calls, or business logic
- ❌ Importing new dependencies for visual effects
- ❌ Creating one-off styled wrappers — use the shared layout primitives
